subject: [PATCH 0/2] fuse: Fix possible memleak at startup with immediate teardown

reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary: Reviewer Horst Birthelmer agrees with the original patch's analysis of the race condition, but suggests checking the fc->connected state under the fc lock before creating the ring to prevent a hang on ring creation vs abort races.
reviews.raw_body:
It does align with what I have seen, even though my testcase only tested
the teardown when the fuse server ran into a problem.
You expanded that quite a bit ;-)

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
Bernd Schubert expressed skepticism about the existence of a registration time leak, stating that he doesn't see any leak even with the additional patch to tear down entries through IO_URING_F_CANCEL.

Reviewer Bernd Schubert pointed out that the described memory leak does not occur in io-uring, but rather is caused by a missing free of 'queue->ent_in_userspace' in fuse_uring_destruct, and suggested simplifying the fix to just freeing the entries in the list without additional complexity.
reviews.raw_body:
I need to go through the other messages, but I still do not see any
registration time leak. At least not with the additional patch we have
here to tear down entries through IO_URING_F_CANCEL


Sorry, besides also looking into ublk now (for main work), also in
progress to fix an issue with reduced queues and also still on the
libfuse part of sync-init....

---

IO-uring sends IO_URING_F_CANCEL for every registred command. We never
had a leak you describe. Upstream has a leak because it does not free
'queue->ent_in_userspace' in fuse_uring_destruct. I'm fine with the
addition in fuse_uring_cancel() (although the just freeing the entries
in the list is much simpler and race free).

Please let's not make it anymore complex.


Thanks,
Bernd

2026-04-11.patch_summary: This patch fixes a possible memory leak in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module by introducing preemptive checking at registration time to prevent races between registration and abort operations. The patch addresses two potential hangs that can occur when there are concurrent registrations and aborts, one due to a race between registration and queue teardown, and another due to a race between ring creation and abort. To fix these issues, the patch introduces additional checks and synchronization mechanisms to ensure that resources are properly released before they are reused or deallocated.
